name: leo_circular_coast_10s
note: Keplerian reference using theta = v_circ*dt/r. The Cowell RK4 integrator applies both Earth gravity (including J2) and Moon third-body perturbation; over 10s these cause at most ~1m deviation from pure Keplerian. Tolerance of 100m is very conservative for this short interval.

name: leo_circular_coast_100s
description: Same circular LEO propagated forward 100 seconds. theta = v_circ*100/r = 7668.64*100/6778000 = 0.113137 rad. cos(0.113137) = 0.993623, sin(0.113137) = 0.112985. x = 6778000*0.993623 = 6734777, y = 6778000*0.112985 = 765812, vx = -7668.64*0.112985 = -866.39, vy = 7668.64*0.993623 = 7619.75. Computed from pure-Keplerian two-body circular orbit formula; J2 and Moon perturbations are excluded from the reference.
note: Analytical Keplerian (two-body, no J2) reference for 100s propagation. The Cowell RK4 integrator includes J2 oblateness and Moon third-body perturbation, which accumulate ~600m of deviation from the pure Keplerian reference over 100s. The 1000m / 5 m/s tolerances detect implementation bugs while accommodating the perturbation gap.

name: leo_circular_coast_one_orbit
description: Same circular LEO propagated forward one approximate orbit period (5550 s). T = 2*pi*sqrt(r^3/MU) = 2*pi*sqrt(3.11384e20/3.986004418e14) = 2*pi*sqrt(780820) = 2*pi*883.6 = 5550.7 s. After dt=5550 s the spacecraft has subtended theta = 0.113137*55.5 = 6.2791 rad ≈ 2*pi - 0.0041 rad: nearly one full revolution. Acceptance criterion: orbital radius and speed within 5 km / 5 m/s of initial values.
note: After one orbit period the spacecraft should nearly return to its starting position. The expected_state here represents the ideal return; the test code uses orbital radius and speed conservation (|r_final - r0| < pos_tol, |v_final - v0| < vel_tol) rather than Cartesian comparison. Tolerance of 5 km / 5 m/s is consistent with TC-INT-4 (energy conservation over 5400 s).

name: moon_llo_circular_coast_100s
note: Analytical Keplerian (point-mass Moon, no J2) reference for lunar orbit. The Cowell integrator includes Earth third-body perturbation (~2.6e-5 m/s^2); over 100s this adds ~0.13m/s velocity perturbation and ~7m position perturbation. Tolerance of 500m / 2 m/s is very conservative.
